source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/04_제품별 인허가 진행 문서/02_ADD (HAD1417MCW)/▶ CE MDD/■ 1. 2차 CE 인허가/Attachment 10. Clinical Evaluation/Attachment 10-2 Clinical Evaluation Report/Clinical Evaluation Report (A-TCF-5-060).docx

document.title: Clinical Evaluation Report for ADD Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

sourceSummary.oneLine: Clinical evaluation report for ADD, a Class IIa digital flat panel X-ray detector, prepared for CE MDD conformity assessment.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Intended for authorized competent authorities and notified bodies of H&abyz Co.,Ltd. as clinical evaluation evidence for safety, performance, benefit-risk, and Essential Requirements under MDD.
keyPoints: ADD is described as a wired or wireless digital flat panel detector for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy in adults and children, not for mammography.
The device is classified as Class IIa by Rule 16 of Annex IX under MDD 93/42/EEC as amended by Directive 2007/47/EEC.
Clinical evaluation was performed using literature for equivalent devices, PMS documentation, risk management documentation, and pre-clinical/bench evidence.
The report concludes that risks associated with device use are acceptable when weighed against patient benefits.
Sales approval status for South Korea, EU, U.S.A., and China is listed as TBD.

concepts.name: Clinical evaluation
concepts.evidence: Clinical evaluation is regarded as an ongoing process conducted throughout the life cycle of a medical device.

concepts.name: MDD Essential Requirements
concepts.evidence: The conformity to the following Essential Requirements (MDD 93/42/EEC Annex I) is assessed: Essential Requirement 1: Safety Requirements Essential Requirement 3: Performance Requirements Essential Requirement 6: Side-effect Requirements

concepts.name: Demonstration of equivalence
concepts.evidence: We have demonstrated equivalence according to the Demonstration of Equivalence requirements of MEDDEV 2.7 / 1 Rev.4

concepts.name: Post Market Surveillance
concepts.evidence: Manufacturer will be performing the PMS&PMCF activities after CE marked.

concepts.name: Post Market Clinical Follow-up
concepts.evidence: Through continuous PMS activity, the influence of the clinical performance and safety related to the device is constantly grasped in various aspects. In addition, PMCF studies will be conducted in accordance with the PMCF Plan if necessary.

concepts.name: Detective quantum efficiency
concepts.evidence: The DQE has been used to describe the imaging performance of an X-ray imaging device.

concepts.name: Risk management
concepts.evidence: Therefore, this clinical evaluation is part on the risk management process according to EN ISO 14971:2012.

evidence.claim: The report is Clinical Evaluation Report A-TCF-5-060 Rev.2 for ADD Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector.
evidence.evidenceText: Clinical Evaluation Report for ADD Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Report No.: A-TCF-5-060 (Rev.2)

evidence.claim: The Rev.2 change was to revise the product name and is dated 2021.04.02.
evidence.evidenceText: 3 2 Revise the product name 2021.04.02

evidence.claim: ADD is classified as Class IIa by Rule 16 of Annex IX under MDD 93/42/EEC as amended by Directive 2007/47/EEC.
evidence.evidenceText: Classification Class IIa by Rule 16 of Annex IX, MDD 93/42/EEC as amended by Directive 2007/47/EEC.
evidence.pageTarget: ADD Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

evidence.claim: ADD is intended for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy in adults and children and is not for mammography.
evidence.evidenceText: ADD(Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector), Digital Flat Panel X-Ray Detector is indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children... Not to be used for mammography.

evidence.claim: The device is not disposable, not invasive, and not implantable.
evidence.evidenceText: Disposable Device N/A It is not disposable device Invasive Device N/A It is not invasive device Implantable Device N/A It is not implantable device

evidence.claim: The target patient population is adults and children except infants and pregnant women.
evidence.evidenceText: Target Patient Population A Adult and children except infant and pregnant women

evidence.claim: Contraindications include mammography, direct cardiac application, infant use, and pregnant women.
evidence.evidenceText: Not to be used for mammography and direct cardiac application. Not to be used for infant and pregnant women.

evidence.claim: ADD uses a-Si TFT sensor technology with CsI scintillator.
evidence.evidenceText: Sensor Type a-Si TFT Scintillator CsI

evidence.claim: ADD supports wired or wireless digital radiography operation and auto-trigger signal sensing without generator integration.
evidence.evidenceText: The ADD are wired or wireless digital flat panel detectors... The detectors support an auto-trigger signal sensing technology that allows the detectors to be used without generator integration.

evidence.claim: The report considered EN ISO 14971:2012 as the risk management standard.
evidence.evidenceText: EN ISO 14971:2012 Medical devices – Application of risk management to medical devices

evidence.claim: The report considered MEDDEV 2.7/1 Rev.4 for clinical evaluation.
evidence.evidenceText: MEDDEV 2.7/1 Rev.4 Clinical Evaluation

evidence.claim: The report considered IEC 62220-1-1:2015 for digital X-ray imaging device detector DQE.
evidence.evidenceText: IEC 62220-1-1:2015 Medical electrical equipment - Characteristics of digital X-ray imaging devices - Part 1-1: Determination of the detective quantum efficiency – Detectors used in radiographic imaging

evidence.claim: Clinical evaluation was based on currently available scientific literature for equivalent products, not clinical investigations.
evidence.evidenceText: Based on scientific literature currently available A Clinical evaluations were performed using the currently available scientific literatures on equivalent products. Based on Clinical investigations made N/A N/A

evidence.claim: The report asserts equivalence with DRX-1, CXDI-31, CXDI-70C, CXDI-11, and S4335AW.
evidence.evidenceText: We used as the clinical evaluation data the follow device, -DRX-1, CXDI-31, CXDI-70C, CXDI-11, S4335AW

evidence.claim: Literature search used PubMed and Science Direct and covered searches from 2020.01.05 to 2020.06.15.
evidence.evidenceText: The “PUBMED” and “Science Direct” were utilized to search for literature... Date of Search: 2020.01.05 ~ 2020.06.15

evidence.claim: The report concluded that the device can be expected to exhibit claimed technical performance and potential clinical effects.
evidence.evidenceText: Considering the literature review, safety & performance test and comparison to similar devices, we concluded that this device can be expected to exhibit the claimed technical performance and potential clinical effects.

evidence.claim: The report concluded that serious side effects were not found in similar devices and residual risk was acceptable.
evidence.evidenceText: According to the clinical data, the serious side effects is not found in the similar device. As well as the overall residual risk has been proven to be acceptable through data analysis sufficiently and risk management

evidence.claim: Sales approval status for South Korea, EU, U.S.A., and China is TBD.
evidence.evidenceText: Country License Status South Korea TBD EU TBD U.S.A. TBD China TBD

evidence.claim: The report states there was no sales record during the PMS review period.
evidence.evidenceText: Customer Complaints Analysis There is no sales record during this period Adverse Events There is no sales record during the period.

evidence.claim: The next clinical evaluation period is listed as 2020.08.01 to 2021.08.01 for CE Surveillance audit.
evidence.evidenceText: The next clinical evaluation 2020.08.01. ~ 2021.08.01. CE Surveillance audit

reviewFlags.reason: Document dates are inconsistent: cover approval and Rev.2 revision history show 2021.04.02/APR/02/2021, while evaluator and final release signatures show 2020.09.01.
Authority is inferred from cited MDD and EU directives; no notified body name or certificate number is provided in the extracted text.
Sales approval status for South Korea, EU, U.S.A., and China is listed as TBD, so market authorization status is not established.
Source contains apparent numbering and text quality issues, including '#005 Literature' labeled as '#004 Literature' and repeated/incomplete text such as 'The'.

There is conflicting technical wording for Hauwere et al.; one section describes amorphous silicon flat-panel technology as indirect energy conversion, while another calls it direct energy conversion.
Product signal HAD/A/F 1417-1717 appears only in metadata and not in the document text; product model extraction is limited to ADD.
